description: On Fitbit activity-tracker devices, certain addresses never change. According to the popets-2019-0036.pdf document, this leads to "permanent trackability" and "considerable privacy concerns" without a user-accessible anonymization feature. The devices, such as Charge 2, transmit Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) advertising packets with a TxAdd flag indicating random addresses, but the addresses remain constant. If devices come within BLE range at one or more locations where an adversary has set up passive sniffing, the adversary can determine whether the same device has entered one of these locations.
Claude Reasoning: This is a wrist-worn wearable (smartwatch/fitness watch); although such devices can track sleep, they are personal mobile devices not deployed within the residence, and the project scopes sleep trackers to bedside/in-bed monitors - so wearables are out of scope.
